_comment: Register person-GUID → the one declarant name that person's profile is published under. register.cacbg.bg re-spells declarants between folder years; scripts/officials/shared.ts canonicalDeclarantName() levels the mechanical drift (case, inner whitespace, hyphen spacing, a 'д-р' prefix), which folds 233 of the 270 forked people back together. What is left over is here: 36 register people whose spellings differ by more than that — a genuine typo ('Натгалия', 'Симееон', 'Маритн'), a swapped patronymic and surname, or a real name change (marriage, divorce, a legal rename). No textual rule can close those without also merging two same-named strangers, so they are resolved by the register's OWN per-person id instead. Keyed on the GUID because it is stable across every filing one declarant ever makes, including across institutions and across the exec/municipal tiers. Both ingests substitute `name` for the register's listing name before slugging AND in the index row, so one person shows one name.
_whatBelongsHere: ONLY a GUID proven to be ONE person under two or more spellings — which the GUID itself proves, since register.cacbg.bg issues it per declarant. The danger runs the other way from scripts/officials/_slug_collisions.json: listing a GUID here MERGES spellings, so an entry whose `name` collides with a DIFFERENT person's canonical name under the same institution publishes one official's property under another's name. Before adding, check that no second GUID already sits on the target slug — the corpus gate in scripts/officials/officials_slug.data.test.ts fails if one does. EDDF7B29… is the worked example of a case that does NOT belong here; see _notListed.
_howToAdd: Run the officials ingest, or `npx vitest run scripts/officials/officials_slug.data.test.ts`. The corpus gate pins how many register people are still forked WITHIN one institution; when it rises, a new spelling has landed. Find it by grouping data/officials/declarations by the person-GUID in each filing's sourceUrl (scripts/officials/slug_identity.ts personGuid()), take the spelling from the most recent declaration year as `name`, list the ones it replaces in `folds`, and say why in `reason`. Prefer the newest spelling: it is the person's current legal name, and a marriage rename should not leave the profile under the maiden name. Do NOT strip a trailing digit as though it were noise — see _notListed and the TITLE_PREFIX note in scripts/officials/shared.ts.

_notListed.EDDF7B29-447A-4C2B-A359-78366EFBAF43: Иван Георгиев Иванов / …Иванов1, Училища. Folding the digit suffix here would NOT just reunite one person: the bare-named slug ivan-georgiev-ivanov-b749dc already carries a SECOND register person (2E6D233C…), so the merge would pull a stranger's property into the profile. Resolving it means deciding which of the two GUIDs is the second person and listing it in ./_slug_collisions.json — a call that needs both declarations opened and compared, per that file's _howToAdd. Left forked until then.
